---
title: "My Analysis Title"
description: "One-sentence description of what this analysis investigates"
date: 2025-03-01
categories: [Analysis, ggplot2, Modelling]
image: "../assets/thumbnails/analysis-one.png"  # optional
toc: true
code-fold: true
---

## Question

State the question or problem this analysis addresses in 1–2 sentences.

## Data

Describe the dataset and load it here.

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(here)

# Load your data
# data <- read_csv(here("data", "your-data.csv"))

# For demo, use a built-in dataset
data <- palmerpenguins::penguins |> drop_na()

glimpse(data)
```

## Exploration

```{r}
#| label: eda
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of key variable"

data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = bill_length_mm, fill = species)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#1B6CA8", "#E26A2C", "#2CA85A")) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Bill Length by Species",
    x = "Bill length (mm)",
    y = "Count",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

## Analysis

Describe your approach, then show the code.

```{r}
#| label: model

model <- lm(body_mass_g ~ bill_length_mm + flipper_length_mm + species,
            data = data)

broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) |>
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 2, caption = "Model coefficients")
```

## Key Findings

- **Finding 1** — one sentence explaining what you found
- **Finding 2** — one sentence
- **Finding 3** — one sentence

## Conclusion

Summarise the takeaways in a short paragraph. Link to any follow-up work if relevant.

---

*Source code: [GitHub](https://github.com/yourusername/your-analysis)*
